title: Phase 2: Implement database layer (BudgetEntity, migration, modified DAOs)

featureSpec.concept: Separate wallets - each budget is an independent container with its own transactions, recurring transactions, and summary
featureSpec.defaultBehavior: After migration or fresh install, user can create additional budgets. All screens filter by active budget.
featureSpec.migration: On first launch after update, if existing data exists, show a prompt to import existing budget into a named budget. User fills a budget details card (name, description, currency, target) to migrate their existing data.
featureSpec.navigation: Top bar dropdown for quick budget switching, always visible

migrationSteps: Insert default 'My Budget' if existing data exists
Add budget_id to transactions with DEFAULT 0
Add budget_id to recurring_transactions with DEFAULT 0
Update all existing rows to reference default budget
plan.summary: Detailed implementation plan for adding multiple named budgets (wallets) to the Android budget manager app, covering database migration, new BudgetEntity/DAO/Repository/ViewModel layers, ActiveBudgetManager singleton for budget switching via DataStore-backed StateFlow, budget-scoped filtering across all existing DAOs and ViewModels, top-bar dropdown UI, budget management screen, migration dialog for existing users, and backup/restore compatibility.
